goal: wave14-v2-personalization: Ship SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 14 (Phase 4 personalization depth + Phase 2 community types + matching experiments) per docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md. Wave 14 builds on Wave 13 (merged to main): unified object store + visibility (Wave 12), Phase 1 community types fully wired end to end with session/Bearer parity, Ask Submit/Find Help write parity, and Phase 2 community types present only as reserved API stubs + a no-op match stub. Wave 14 delivers THREE workstreams: (1) PHASE 2 COMMUNITY TYPES — promote question, mentorship_request, project_request, collaboration_offer, apprenticeship_opportunity from reserved-draft-only stubs to fully wired community types: real type-specific fields, create/publish (per publish preference)/list/detail, and their action verbs (reply, follow, save, offer_help, request_mentor, ask_for_intro, apply, ask_for_intro, etc.) working end to end with visibility enforced and session/Bearer parity, plus human UI on .org. (2) MATCHING EXPERIMENTS — replace the Wave 13 no-op match stub with a real, clearly-labeled EXPERIMENTAL matcher: given a community object (e.g. help_request, mentorship_request, project_request), compute ranked candidate matches from other community objects/offers using a simple deterministic rule-based scorer (tag/skill/topic overlap + type compatibility) with human-readable reasons, persist match runs + thumbs feedback, callable identically via session cookie and Bearer token, and surfaced in the .org UI as an experimental Match action with candidate cards + feedback. (3) PERSONALIZATION v2 — deepen the rule-based recommendations: wire bookmarks into the .io article ranker, implement .io Insights personal relevance reordering for logged-in users, and replace the .org dashboard's pseudo/hashed activity signals with REAL signals (followed topics, contributions, community interactions) sourced from a new backend activity-summary endpoint. English-only UI, light theme default + dark toggle, content-first editorial per project skills (.agents/skills/editorial-ui + ux-copy). Extend Wave 12/13 infra — do NOT rebuild the object store, visibility filter, community lane, comment model, or the typed shared API client.
summary: Jacky's bot: shipping SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 14 — Phase 2 community types fully wired (.org UI + Agent API parity), a real experimental Match capability, and personalization v2 (bookmarks-into-ranking, .io Insights relevance, .org real activity signals)
acceptanceCriteria: Backend builds + `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` passes on the integrated branch; both web apps build (`pnpm turbo build`)

Phase 2 community types (question, mentorship_request, project_request, collaboration_offer, apprenticeship_opportunity) are no longer reserved-draft-only: they can be created with type-specific fields, published per publish preference, listed/detailed with visibility enforced, and their action verbs work end to end on .org with session/Bearer parity

The Match action is a real EXPERIMENTAL matcher (not a no-op stub): it returns ranked candidate matches with human-readable reasons for an eligible community object, persists match runs + thumbs feedback, is callable identically via session cookie and Bearer token, and renders in the .org UI clearly labeled as experimental

Personalization v2: bookmarks feed the .io article ranker; .io Insights reorders by onboarding profile (and assessment) for logged-in users; the .org logged-in dashboard uses REAL activity signals (followed topics, contributions, interactions) from a backend activity-summary endpoint instead of pseudo/hashed signals
Agentic Access API docs (/for-agents) on both sites document the now-active Phase 2 community types + the experimental matching endpoint (Bearer /api/v1) and parity with on-site actions
One integrated draft PR to main; docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to mark Wave 14 shipped

tasks.name: shared-foundation
tasks.scopedGoal: Add the Wave 14 shared contracts to @ai-transformation/shared (packages/shared ONLY) for three workstreams: Phase 2 community types, a real experimental matcher, and personalization v2 signals. BUILD ON TOP of the existing modules — do NOT duplicate or fork what already exists in packages/shared/src/wave12-objects.ts (communityObjectTypeSchema with Phase 1 types discussion|help_request|event|community_announcement and Phase 2 types question|mentorship_request|project_request|collaboration_offer|apprenticeship_opportunity, objectRecordSchema, visibility, contribution*, comment*, personal layer, profile, publishPreference), wave13-community.ts (communityActionVerbSchema, communityPhase1ActionTaxonomy, communityPhase2ReservedActionTaxonomy, getCommunityActions, isCommunityPhase2ReservedType, the reserved/no-op match stub schemas communityReservedNoopSchema/communityReservedTypeResultSchema/communityMatchRequestSchema/communityMatchResponseSchema), recommendation.ts (scoreRecommendation, RecommendationSignals, RecommendationWeights), onboarding.ts (onboardingProfileSchema), or the createApiClient in index.ts (session client + v1 Bearer namespace; existing namespaces objects/contributions/moderation/publishPreference/profile/bookmarks/notes/annotations/comments/recentlyViewed/community plus v1.* mirrors). Add a NEW module packages/shared/src/wave14-community.ts (re-exported from index.ts) that delivers: (1) PHASE 2 TYPE FIELDS — a per-type field schema for each Phase 2 community type capturing its distinctive fields (e.g. question: questionBody, tags[]; mentorship_request: focusArea, seniority, commitment, tags[]; project_request: summary, skillsNeeded[], timeline, tags[]; collaboration_offer: summary, offering[], seeking[], tags[]; apprenticeship_opportunity: summary, focusArea, cohort, tags[]). Keep these as additive optional metadata-style schemas (the object body already stores generic fields; these validate the type-specific extras under object metadata or a typed payload). Export a helper getCommunityTypeFields(type) and a Phase-2-active flag/predicate (e.g. PHASE2_ACTIVE = true / isCommunityTypeActive(type)) so backend + UI agree Phase 2 is now live (not reserved). Keep isCommunityPhase2ReservedType for back-compat but its semantics shift to 'these are the Phase-2 types', and the action taxonomy stays the SAME stable verbs. (2) REAL EXPERIMENTAL MATCHER — schemas that REPLACE the no-op behavior while keeping the old shapes importable for back-compat: matchCandidateSchema { objectId, type, title, score (0..1), reasons: string[] }, matchExperimentRequestSchema { site, objectId, type?, limit? (default 5, max 20) }, matchExperimentResponseSchema { experimental: true, objectId, type, generatedAt, candidates: matchCandidate[], note? }, and matchFeedbackRequestSchema { site, objectId, candidateObjectId, verdict: 'up'|'down' } + matchFeedbackResponseSchema { ok: true, recorded: true }. Mark experimental clearly in field naming/comments. (3) PERSONALIZATION v2 SIGNALS — extend recommendation.ts ADDITIVELY (do not break scoreRecommendation): add a bookmark-affinity signal name + default weight to RecommendationSignals/RecommendationWeights (e.g. bookmarkAffinity) so the .io ranker can fold bookmarks in; and add an activity-summary contract used by .org personalization: activitySummarySchema { followedTopics: {topic,count}[], contributionsCount, interactionsCount, bookmarksCount, recentObjectTypes: {type,count}[], generatedAt } in wave14-community.ts (or a new wave14-personalization.ts). (4) CLIENT — extend createApiClient on BOTH the session client and the v1 Bearer namespace with: community.match(request) and community.matchFeedback(request) using clean REST paths (POST /api/community/match already exists for the stub — UPGRADE it to return the experiment response and add POST /api/community/match/feedback; mirror on /api/v1/community/*), and a personalization (or signals) namespace with getActivitySummary({site}) hitting /api/personal/activity-summary and /api/v1/personal/activity-summary. The session and v1 method shapes MUST be identical. Reuse the existing request helpers (requestSessionJson/requestAgentJson/withJsonBody/buildQuery) and match their style EXACTLY. Run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck` and ensure it passes. Do NOT touch apps/**, packages/content/**, data/**, docs/**, knowledge-base/**.
tasks.brief: Read docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §4 (Ask modes + unified-API note), §5.3 (community Phase 1 vs Phase 2 types + per-type action verbs), §6 (contextual actions), §9 (recommendations engine + inputs: onboarding profile, assessment, bookmarks, recently viewed, curated spotlight; .org emphasizes followed topics / contributions activity / community interactions), §11 (Phase 4 personalization depth) and §12 Wave 14 (Personalization v2, Phase 2 community types, matching experiments). Wave 12 + 13 already shipped the object store, visibility, Phase 1 community actions, the session/Bearer client, and Phase 2 as reserved stubs — your job is the THIN shared layer that (a) turns Phase 2 types from reserved into active typed types, (b) upgrades the match stub schemas into a real experimental-matcher contract (keeping old shapes importable), and (c) adds personalization v2 signal contracts (bookmark affinity weight + an activity-summary shape + client method). This module is the dependency that backend, web-org, and web-io all branch from, so keep exports clean, additive, generic, and well-typed. No backend logic, no UI — schemas + client only. English code/comments.
acceptance: A new wave14 shared module is re-exported from @ai-transformation/shared without duplicating Wave 12/13 object/community/recommendation/client code
Phase 2 community types have per-type field schemas + getCommunityTypeFields(type) and a Phase-2-active predicate/flag; action taxonomy verbs unchanged and stable

Real experimental matcher schemas exist (matchCandidate, matchExperimentRequest/Response with experimental:true, matchFeedback request/response); old reserved/no-op match shapes still importable for back-compat
recommendation.ts gains an additive bookmark-affinity signal+weight (scoreRecommendation still works); an activitySummary contract exists for .org personalization
createApiClient gains identical community.match + community.matchFeedback and a personalization/getActivitySummary method on BOTH session client and v1 Bearer namespace (/api/* and /api/v1/*)
packages/shared typecheck passes; no files outside packages/shared changed

tasks.name: backend-wave14
tasks.scopedGoal: Implement the Wave 14 backend in apps/backend ONLY, satisfying EXACTLY the shared contracts added by shared-foundation (community.match + community.matchFeedback + personalization.getActivitySummary, on BOTH /api/* and /api/v1/* with identical session/Bearer handlers). Build on the existing object store + community lane — do NOT rebuild visibility or the object/comment model. Reuse: apps/backend/src/lanes/objects/index.ts (resolveRequester, toVisibilityContext, requireResolvedUser, the create/draft/submit paths, and the toReservedCommunityMetadata gating for Phase 2 types), apps/backend/src/db/objects.ts (createObject/listObjectsForRequester/getObjectByIdForRequester/submitObject + buildVisibilityFilter + VisibilityQueryContext), apps/backend/src/lanes/community/index.ts (existing listByType/getWithReplies/replies/follows/offers/joins/interactions + the no-op match handler at POST /community/match) and apps/backend/src/db/community.ts (community_follows, community_interactions, upsert/remove helpers), apps/backend/src/db/personal.ts. Deliver THREE workstreams: (1) PHASE 2 TYPES ACTIVE — stop forcing Phase 2 community types to draft-only/reserved: when a Phase 2 type object is created/submitted it must follow the SAME publish/visibility pipeline as Phase 1 (auto-publish per publish preference, members-only/public per author visibility) rather than always status='draft' + reserved metadata. Validate/store the type-specific Phase 2 fields from shared (getCommunityTypeFields) under object metadata or payload. Ensure listByType + getWithReplies serve Phase 2 types with visibility enforced via buildVisibilityFilter. Wire the Phase 2 action verbs onto the existing interaction infra: extend community_interactions kind to also accept 'request_mentor'|'ask_for_intro'|'apply'|'collaborate' (idempotent where natural) and allow reply/follow/save on Phase 2 types. Keep migrations idempotent + additive (PRAGMA table_info / CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS in db/community.ts). (2) REAL EXPERIMENTAL MATCHER — REPLACE the no-op match handler with a real deterministic, rule-based matcher: given {site, objectId, type?, limit}, load the source community object (visibility-checked for the requester), then score other visible community objects of compatible types as candidates using simple signals (shared tags/skills/topic keyword overlap, type compatibility e.g. help_request<->offer/collaboration_offer, mentorship_request<->collaboration_offer/apprenticeship_opportunity, project_request<->collaboration_offer), normalize score to 0..1, attach human-readable reasons[], return top-N as the shared matchExperimentResponse with experimental:true. Add POST /community/match/feedback persisting {objectId, candidateObjectId, verdict} into a new idempotent community_match_feedback table; optionally a community_match_runs table for auditing. Mount on BOTH /api and /api/v1 (the community router is already dual-mounted). Bearer-owner maps to the same users row via getUserByEmail — reuse resolveRequester. NO LLM calls; deterministic rules only. (3) ACTIVITY SUMMARY — add GET /personal/activity-summary (and thus /api/v1/personal/activity-summary) returning the shared activitySummary shape for the resolved user: aggregate followedTopics (from community_follows joined to object tags/type), contributionsCount (user's contributions/objects), interactionsCount (community_interactions by user), bookmarksCount (personal_bookmarks), recentObjectTypes (from recently_viewed or interactions). Authenticated only (session or Bearer); empty/zeroed summary for anonymous. (4) TESTS — extend apps/backend/src/lanes/community/community.test.ts (and/or objects.test.ts) to cover: Phase 2 type create+auto-publish+list+detail+visibility matrix (anonymous/io-member/org-member/owner/bearer-owner), Phase 2 actions (reply/follow/request_mentor/apply), the experimental matcher returns ranked candidates with reasons for a help_request and a mentorship_request and respects visibility (never matches against objects the requester can't see), match feedback persists, activity-summary returns real aggregates, and session/Bearer parity for match + activity-summary. Run `pnpm install` first if node_modules missing, then `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck` and ensure BOTH pass. Do NOT touch apps/web-io/**, apps/web-org/**, apps/combined/**, packages/**, or data/**.
tasks.brief: Read docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §5.3 (Phase 2 types + action verbs incl. Match), §7 (moderation/publish: auto-publish vs review per user/submission; never elevate to public without author choice), §9 (.org personalization signals: followed topics, contributions activity, community interactions), §11 Phase 4, §12 Wave 14. The shared-foundation branch you start from defines the exact contracts (match experiment, match feedback, activity summary, Phase 2 field helpers) — implement handlers that satisfy them on BOTH /api and /api/v1. CRITICAL: Wave 13 deliberately forced Phase 2 community types to draft-only with reserved metadata (toReservedCommunityMetadata in lanes/objects/index.ts and reserved gating in the create/draft/submit paths) and made POST /community/match a no-op returning reserved:true — Wave 14 must REMOVE that gating for Phase 2 types and REPLACE the no-op match with a real deterministic matcher. Route every community read through buildVisibilityFilter so private/members-only never leak (the matcher must only consider candidates the requester can see). Keep migrations idempotent + additive (Wave 12/13 used PRAGMA table_info guards). No LLM — rule-based scoring only (this is the 'experiment', clearly labeled experimental in the response). English code. node_modules are often missing on cloud start — run `pnpm install` first.
acceptance: Phase 2 community types follow the same publish/visibility pipeline as Phase 1 (no longer forced draft-only/reserved); type-specific Phase 2 fields validated/stored; listByType + getWithReplies serve them with visibility enforced

Phase 2 action verbs work via the interaction infra (community_interactions kinds extended for request_mentor/ask_for_intro/apply/collaborate; reply/follow/save on Phase 2 types); migrations idempotent + additive

POST /community/match is a REAL deterministic rule-based matcher returning ranked candidates with reasons + experimental:true, visibility-respecting; POST /community/match/feedback persists thumbs feedback; both mounted on /api and /api/v1
GET /personal/activity-summary returns real aggregates (followed topics, contributions, interactions, bookmarks, recent types) for the resolved user on /api and /api/v1; empty for anonymous
All new writes/reads callable identically via session cookie and Bearer token (same handlers on /api and /api/v1)

community tests cover Phase 2 create/publish/list/visibility + actions + experimental matcher ranking/visibility + match feedback + activity-summary + session/Bearer parity; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and typecheck pass
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- From repo root run `pnpm install` if node_modules missing.
## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck` must pass.
- Confirm the community match + match/feedback + personal/activity-summary handlers are mounted on BOTH /api and /api/v1.
## Manual
- Create a Phase 2 mentorship_request via session AND Bearer; confirm it auto-publishes per preference and appears in listByType with visibility enforced (members-only/private filtered for anonymous/cross-site). Call POST /community/match for a help_request and a mentorship_request; confirm ranked candidates + reasons + experimental:true and that no candidate is one the requester can't see. Submit match feedback (up/down) and confirm it persists idempotently. Call /personal/activity-summary and confirm real non-zero aggregates after follows/contributions/interactions; confirm anonymous gets a zeroed summary.
## Gotchas
- Reuse buildVisibilityFilter (no second visibility path). Bearer-owner maps to the same users row via getUserByEmail. Auto-publish must respect visibility. Matcher is deterministic/rule-based — NO LLM. Keep Phase 1 behavior unchanged.

tasks.name: web-org-wave14
tasks.scopedGoal: Wire the ai-transformation.org Wave 14 UI (apps/web-org ONLY, + data/curated/org-*.json) for Phase 2 community types, the experimental Match action, and personalization v2 dashboard signals, consuming the shared client methods from shared-foundation (use getApiClient().community.* / .personalization.* — do NOT invent shadow fetch shapes). You start from the shared-foundation branch. Deliver: (1) PHASE 2 TYPES LIVE — in components/community-highlights.tsx, components/community-object-view.tsx, app/community/[id]/page.tsx, lib/use-community-interactions.ts, and lib/object-display.ts: stop rendering Phase 2 types (question, mentorship_request, project_request, collaboration_offer, apprenticeship_opportunity) as 'Reserved / coming soon' disabled affordances. Render them as ACTIVE community objects with their real per-type actions enabled (reply/follow/save plus type-appropriate verbs: request_mentor/ask_for_intro for mentorship_request, apply for project_request/apprenticeship_opportunity, offer_help/collaborate for collaboration_offer/help_request). Use the shared Phase-2-active predicate from shared (no longer isCommunityPhase2ReservedType-as-disabled). Keep the sample-card fallback when there are no live objects. (2) EXPERIMENTAL MATCH UI — add a clearly-labeled experimental 'Find matches' / 'Match me' action on eligible community objects (help_request, mentorship_request, project_request, collaboration_offer) in community-object-view.tsx (and optionally a compact entry on highlights): call getApiClient().community.match({site,objectId,type}) and render the ranked candidate cards (title, score, reasons[]) with a clear 'Experimental' badge + a short explanation that matches are rule-based suggestions, plus thumbs up/down feedback wired to community.matchFeedback. Handle empty/loading/guest (sign-in prompt) states. (3) PERSONALIZATION v2 DASHBOARD — replace the pseudo/hashed signals in lib/home-recommendations.ts + lib/recommendation-types.ts with REAL data: fetch getApiClient().personalization.getActivitySummary({site:'org'}) and feed followedTopics/contributionsCount/interactionsCount into the ActivitySignal scoring (scoreRecommendation), and update components/community-activity-dashboard.tsx so the contributions/interactions/followed-topics panels show real counts/items instead of placeholder copy. Keep a graceful fallback when the summary is empty (new user). (4) Update apps/web-org/app/for-agents/page.tsx to document Phase 2 community types as now ACTIVE (creatable/listable with type fields + actions) and the experimental matching endpoint (POST /api/v1/community/match + /match/feedback, Bearer), noting parity with on-site actions; remove 'reserved'/'coming soon' framing for Phase 2 (keep an 'experimental' note for Match). (5) Update data/curated/org-home.json + data/curated/org-hub-index.json copy/CTAs if any reference Phase-2-reserved or Wave-14-pending community features. Ensure the web-org build/typecheck succeeds (`pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck`). Do NOT touch apps/web-io/**, packages/**, apps/backend/**, apps/combined/**.
tasks.brief: READ FIRST: docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §2 (.org positioning), §3.3 (.org route map — /community public-only for guests vs full opportunity layer for members), §4 (Ask modes), §5.3 (community Phase 1/Phase 2 types + per-type action verbs incl. Match), §6 (contextual actions), §9 (.org personalization emphasizes followed topics / contributions / interactions), §11 Phase 4, §12 Wave 14. LOAD the project UI skills before building UI: .agents/skills/editorial-ui/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/ux-copy/SKILL.md (content-first editorial, English-only UI, light default + dark toggle). Existing wiring to reuse (Wave 13): community-highlights.tsx, community-object-view.tsx (has a ReservedActions component + 'reserved/coming soon' rendering you must replace for Phase 2), app/community/[id]/page.tsx, lib/use-community-interactions.ts, lib/object-display.ts (COMMUNITY_TYPE_LABEL/COMMUNITY_VERB_LABEL incl. match:'Match'), components/community-activity-dashboard.tsx + lib/home-recommendations.ts + lib/recommendation-types.ts (currently use pseudoSignal() hashing — replace with real activity summary), lib/use-auth-user.ts (guest/member), lib/api-client.ts getApiClient(). You depend ONLY on shared-foundation; code against the typed shared client contract (the backend implements it in parallel) — your build/typecheck must pass against the client interface even though the live backend integrates later. Label Match clearly as EXPERIMENTAL. NO legacy redirects. Capture before/after screenshots or a short screen recording of Phase 2 type actions, the Match candidates UI, and the dashboard real signals, and reference the artifact path in your handoff.
acceptance: Phase 2 community types render as ACTIVE objects with real per-type actions (no longer 'reserved/coming soon'); sample fallback retained when empty

An experimental, clearly-labeled Match action calls community.match and renders ranked candidate cards (title/score/reasons) with thumbs feedback via community.matchFeedback; guest sees sign-in prompt; loading/empty handled
community-activity-dashboard + home-recommendations use REAL activity-summary signals (followed topics, contributions, interactions) instead of pseudo/hashed signals, with graceful new-user fallback
.org /for-agents documents Phase 2 types as active + the experimental matching endpoints (Bearer /api/v1) and on-site parity; reserved/coming-soon framing removed for Phase 2
data/curated/org-*.json copy/CTAs updated where they referenced Phase-2-reserved or Wave-14-pending features
web-org build + typecheck pass; all UI consumes the shared client (no shadow fetch)
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Build web-org with the repo build (`pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build`) and typecheck.
## Automated
- Build + type-check must pass. Confirm Phase 2 types are no longer rendered via the reserved/coming-soon path and that Match uses getApiClient().community.match (no shadow fetch).
## Manual
- Run web-org dev: open /community and a Phase 2 object detail; confirm real actions render (not 'coming soon'); trigger Match and confirm experimental candidate cards + reasons + thumbs feedback; sign out and confirm guest sign-in prompt; open the logged-in Home dashboard and confirm real followed-topics/contributions/interactions counts (not hashed placeholders). Confirm /for-agents documents Phase 2 active + experimental matching.
## Gotchas
- Use the shared client only; the backend integrates later so build against the contract. English-only UI, light default + dark toggle. Label Match EXPERIMENTAL.

tasks.name: web-io-wave14
tasks.scopedGoal: Deliver the ai-transformation.io personalization v2 depth (apps/web-io ONLY, + data/curated/io-*.json if copy needs it), consuming the shared recommendation signals/weights and client from shared-foundation. You start from the shared-foundation branch. Deliver: (1) BOOKMARKS INTO RANKING — fold the user's bookmarks into the .io article ranker (lib/recommendations.ts rankArticles): add a bookmark-affinity signal (e.g. pillar/tag overlap with bookmarked library items, using the new shared bookmarkAffinity signal+weight) so logged-in users' recommendations reflect what they've saved. Source bookmarks via the existing client (getApiClient().bookmarks / lib/bookmarks.ts) and resolve bookmarked slugs to pillars/tags like recently-viewed already does. Keep logged-out behavior unchanged. (2) INSIGHTS PERSONAL RELEVANCE — implement the currently-missing logged-in personalization on /insights: components/insights-cards.tsx must reorder/filter the IO_INSIGHTS cards by the user's onboarding profile (role/industry, via useOnboardingProfile) and assessment weakest-gap when signed in, with a clear 'Personalized for your role' affordance; logged-out stays the static curated order. Add a small lib/insights-recommendations.ts (rule-based scorer reusing scoreRecommendation/the shared signals) rather than bespoke ad-hoc sorting. The /insights PageIntro already promises 'Sign in to reorder by your role and industry' — make that real. (3) DASHBOARD POLISH — update components/personal-dashboard.tsx so the recommendations reflect the richer signals (bookmarks + assessment + profile + recently viewed) and clearly explain WHY items are recommended (short reason chips), staying content-first per editorial-ui. Keep it rule-based (NO LLM ranking — that remains deferred per §9). Ensure `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io typecheck` pass. Do NOT touch apps/web-org/**, packages/**, apps/backend/**, apps/combined/**.
tasks.brief: READ FIRST: docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §2 (.io positioning: public traffic driver + personal cockpit when logged in), §3.3 (.io route map), §9 (recommendations engine: single rule-based engine; inputs = onboarding profile, assessment weakest gap, bookmarks + recently viewed, curated spotlight; NO LLM ranking in Phase 1 — defer), §10 (Insights: logged-in reorder/filter by onboarding profile), §11 Phase 4, §12 Wave 14. LOAD .agents/skills/editorial-ui/SKILL.md + .agents/skills/ux-copy/SKILL.md before UI work (content-first editorial, English-only, light default + dark toggle). Existing wiring to reuse: lib/recommendations.ts rankArticles (signals keywordMatch/curatedBoost/affinity/gapBoost — ADD bookmarkAffinity), lib/assessment-gaps.ts (WEAKEST_GAP_SLUGS), lib/onboarding-profile.ts useOnboardingProfile, lib/recently-viewed.ts, lib/bookmarks.ts (currently NOT in the ranker — wire it in), components/personal-dashboard.tsx (logged-in Home, already calls rankArticles + pulls assessment weakestGap), components/insights-cards.tsx (currently STATIC — add personalization), app/insights/page.tsx, lib/api-client.ts getApiClient(). You depend ONLY on shared-foundation; build/typecheck against the typed shared client + signals contract. Keep everything rule-based. Capture before/after screenshots of the logged-in Home recommendations (with bookmark influence) and the personalized Insights ordering, and reference the artifact path in your handoff.

personal-dashboard reflects the richer signals and shows short reason chips explaining recommendations; remains rule-based (no LLM)
web-io build + typecheck pass; UI consumes the shared client + shared recommendation signals (no bespoke shadow scoring)
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Build web-io (`pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build`) and typecheck.
## Automated
- Build + type-check must pass. Confirm rankArticles now folds bookmarks and insights-cards uses the new rule-based personalization for logged-in users.
## Manual
- Run web-io dev signed in with some bookmarks + an onboarding profile + assessment result: confirm Home recommendations shift toward bookmarked pillars and show reason chips; open /insights and confirm cards reorder for your role/industry with a personalized affordance; sign out and confirm /insights returns to static curated order and Home shows the logged-out content.
## Gotchas
- Rule-based only (LLM ranking is deferred per §9). English-only UI, light default + dark toggle. Don't regress logged-out SEO content.

tasks.name: integrate-wave14
tasks.scopedGoal: Produce the single integrated Wave 14 deliverable branch and open ONE draft PR to main. You start from the backend-wave14 branch (which includes the shared-foundation commits as common ancestor). Merge `orch/wave14-v2-personalization/web-org-wave14` and `orch/wave14-v2-personalization/web-io-wave14` into the current branch. Conflicts should be minimal because the three impl branches touch disjoint trees (apps/backend, apps/web-org + data/curated/org-*.json, apps/web-io + data/curated/io-home.json) over an identical packages/shared common ancestor; if conflicts arise in packages/shared or docs, keep ALL three sides working against the shared Wave 14 contracts (do not drop the backend handlers, the .org Phase 2/Match/dashboard UI, or the .io personalization). After merging: run `pnpm install`, then `pnpm turbo build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` from repo root; fix any integration-only breakage (import drift, type mismatches between backend and the web apps' use of the shared community/match/personalization client). Then, as a LIGHT doc-only change, update apps/web-io/app/for-agents/page.tsx to note that Phase 2 community types are now active and the experimental matching endpoint is available via the shared Agentic Access API (/api/v1, Bearer) — brief, since .io has no community UI. Update docs to reflect Wave 14 shipped: docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md and docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md (note: Phase 2 community types fully wired human UI + Agent API on .org with session/Bearer parity, a real experimental rule-based Match capability with feedback, personalization v2 = bookmarks-into-ranking + .io Insights relevance + .org real activity signals via /personal/activity-summary; visibility still enforced via Wave 12 buildVisibilityFilter; Wave 15+ = newsletter public archive + agent credits if still desired). Commit, push exactly your branch, and open a DRAFT PR to main titled 'Wave 14: SITE_DESIGN_v2 Phase 4 personalization + Phase 2 community types + matching experiments' summarizing the backend (Phase 2 active, experimental matcher, activity-summary), the .org UI (Phase 2 actions, Match cards, real dashboard signals), the .io personalization (bookmarks-in-ranking, Insights relevance), and the Agent API parity. Do NOT merge the PR or mark it ready.
tasks.brief: This is a merge + integration + docs task (planners do not code, so this worker performs the merge). The three impl branches are disjoint by directory; the main risk is divergent use of the shared Wave 14 client contracts (community.match/matchFeedback, personalization.getActivitySummary, Phase 2 field helpers) — reconcile at the type level so `pnpm turbo build` passes for backend + both web apps and backend tests stay green. Read the upstream handoffs (pasted into your prompt) to learn each branch's actual deliverable branch and any deviations. Keep merge history with all parents. English docs/code. The .io /for-agents edit is documentation only (no community UI on .io). After build+test green, update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md, then open the draft PR via the ManagePullRequest flow against main. node_modules are often missing on cloud start — run `pnpm install` first.
acceptance: web-org-wave14 and web-io-wave14 branches merged into the deliverable branch with parents preserved in history
pnpm turbo build passes for backend + web-io + web-org
pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test passes (incl. the new Phase 2 / matcher / activity-summary tests)
.io /for-agents notes Phase 2 active + experimental matching available via /api/v1 Bearer (light doc-only)
docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to reflect Wave 14 shipped
One DRAFT PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready) summarizing Phase 2 types, the experimental matcher, personalization v2, and Agent API parity
tasks.verify:

## Manual

## Gotchas

tasks.name: verify-wave14
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the integrated Wave 14 branch against the planner acceptance criteria and SITE_DESIGN_v2.md Phase 4 / Wave 14. Run `pnpm install`, `pnpm turbo build` (backend + both web apps), and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`. Confirm with execution evidence: (a) Phase 2 community types (question, mentorship_request, project_request, collaboration_offer, apprenticeship_opportunity) are no longer reserved-draft-only — they can be created with type fields, auto-publish per preference, list/detail with visibility enforced, and their actions work (inspect backend lanes/community + lanes/objects for removal of the reserved-draft gating, and the new community tests pass); (b) the Match endpoint is a REAL deterministic matcher (not a no-op stub) returning ranked candidates with reasons + experimental:true, visibility-respecting, with feedback persisted, mounted on /api and /api/v1 with session/Bearer parity; (c) the .org UI renders Phase 2 types as active with real actions and an experimental Match UI (candidate cards + reasons + thumbs feedback) via getApiClient().community.* (no shadow fetch), and the logged-in dashboard uses real activity-summary signals instead of pseudo/hashed ones; (d) .io personalization v2: bookmarks feed rankArticles and /insights reorders by onboarding profile + assessment for logged-in users (rule-based, no LLM); (e) /for-agents on both sites documents Phase 2 active + the experimental matching endpoint; (f) docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md mark Wave 14 shipped and a single draft PR exists. Report execution evidence per criterion; do not pattern-match. If the env blocks a live UI check, run the build + backend tests + route/mount/code-inspection checks and mark unit-test-verified or verifier-blocked honestly.
acceptance: Verification section includes execution evidence (build + backend test output + router-mount + code/route inspection) for every integrate-wave14 acceptance criterion and the planner-level Phase 2-active, experimental-matcher, and personalization-v2 claims
